quotes.text: You can dress up a lie in a hundred-dollar suit and still smell it from across the room.
quotes.speaker: L.A. Confidential (1997)
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — it nails the film's noirish verbal swagger and Bud's blunt, physical way of seeing the world. The subtle giveaway is that the metaphor is a touch too refined for Bud, who is the film's bruiser; this sounds more like Ed Exley's calculating voice dressed up in Bud's clothing.

quotes.text: I feel the need... the need for speed!
quotes.speaker: Top Gun (1986)
quotes.context: Maverick (Tom Cruise) and Goose (Anthony Edwards) say it together, high-fiving as they get pumped up to fly.

quotes.text: One does not simply walk into Mordor.
quotes.speaker: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001)
quotes.context: Boromir (Sean Bean) arguing against the plan to destroy the Ring

quotes.text: A man who won't die for something has already stopped living.
quotes.speaker: Braveheart (1995)
quotes.context: William Wallace rallying his men before battle
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — it matches Wallace's rousing, aphoristic speech style and the film's recurring theme of freedom over survival. The subtle giveaway is that it's a shade too tidy and philosophical, almost like a bumper sticker, whereas Gibson's actual lines tend to be rawer and more defiant.

quotes.text: She never asked me to be better. She just acted like I already was.
quotes.speaker: As Good as It Gets (1997)
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — it echoes the film's emotional core and Melvin's clumsy, backhanded way of expressing love, and it's close in rhythm to the famous 'you make me want to be a better man' line. The giveaway is that it's a little too graceful; Melvin's actual epiphanies tend to arrive awkwardly, surrounded by insults or non-sequiturs.

quotes.speaker: The Shining (1980)
